sanskrit: अविभक्तं च भूतेषु विभक्तमिव च स्थितम् । भूतभर्तृ च तज्ज्ञेयं ग्रसिष्णु प्रभविष्णु च ॥
hindiTranslation: वे परमात्मा स्वयं विभागरहित होते हुए भी सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंमें विभक्तकी तरह स्थित हैं ।  वे जाननेयोग्य परमात्मा ही सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंको उत्पन्न करनेवाले, उनका भरणपोषण करनेवाले और संहार करनेवाले हैं ॥
englishTranslation: Undivided among beings, it appears divided. Know it as the one who bears, devours, and creates all beings.
essence: Separation is only appearance; the many are held by one.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: The supreme reality seems split into many beings, but it is actually one. It is the source, support, and end of all life.
simpleInsight: The feeling of separation softens. What seemed isolated is held inside one larger reality.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna is frozen between duty and grief. Krishna explains that the divine is not fragmented by the many lives it animates. What seems separate is one reality appearing in many forms, and that reality sustains, consumes, and creates all beings.
modernRelevance: You look across a family table after an ugly argument and see only sides. This verse says the deeper truth is still one shared field, even when everyone appears divided.
translationLiteral: अविभक्तम् (undivided) / च (and) / भूतेषु (among beings) / विभक्तम् (divided) / इव (as if) / च (and) / स्थितम् (situated) / भूतभर्तृ (bearer of beings) / च (and) / तत् (that) / ज्ञेयम् (to be known) / ग्रसिष्णु (devouring) / प्रभविष्णु (originating) / च (and)
